text.title: TypeScript Utility and Advanced Types
text.summary: Built-in utility types, template literal types, conditional types, and indexed access patterns for precise TypeScript type expressions.

rule.statement: Use built-in utility types rather than rewriting equivalent mapped types by hand.
rule.rationale: Partial, Required, Readonly, Pick, Omit, Record, keyof, and indexed access are universally understood and compiler-optimized.

rule.statement: Use template literal types to enforce string shape contracts at the type level.
rule.rationale: Template literal types catch mismatched string patterns (e.g., wrong URL prefix) at compile time with zero runtime cost.
